diagnosis: The strategy computes the classic ATR-based SuperTrend indicator from scratch at every bar (lookback capped at 200 bars) and fires only on a direction FLIP (dir_now != dir_prev). It uses three gatekeeping filters before accepting a flip: a regime gate (trend or breakout only), an ADX floor (adx_min=17.2), and a volume ratio floor (volume_ratio >= 1.06). Evolved params include a wide multiplier (3.9) and a generous take-profit multiplier (5.8x ATR) relative to a 3.0x ATR stop-loss. Time stop is 48 hours. The strategy emits BOTH "long" and "short" sides — lines 132-146 of supertrend.py build a short Signal when dir_now == -1. The current code is therefore NOT Phase-3 compliant. In Phase-2 evolutionary runs (36,000 evaluations over 14 hours) the strategy produced only 0.3% positive-fitness genomes before being pruned from the STRATEGY_REGISTRY. The survived genome achieved Sharpe=1.20 (walk-forward 1.10) with only 69 trades, suggesting the edge is thin and trade count is insufficient for reliable statistics. Core weakness: SuperTrend direction flips are inherently a lagging trend-entry/reversal signal. On hourly crypto data the signal arrives well after the impulsive move, and the wide ATR multiplier (3.9) reduces flip frequency so much that the strategy is essentially inactive during choppy regimes and arrives late during fast trends. The 0.3% positive-fitness rate from 36K evaluations is a strong red flag that the raw signal does not carry a consistently exploitable edge in this market structure.
long_only_hypothesis: SuperTrend bullish flips (direction from -1 to +1) tend to coincide with sustained uptrend resumptions in strongly trending crypto markets: price has reclaimed a dynamic ATR-based support band, short-term volatility has compressed, and momentum is accelerating. The long-only variant enters only on bullish flips when ADX confirms trend strength (not range), volume confirms participation, and the macro regime is trend or breakout. Instead of shorting bearish flips, the strategy exits to cash (treat short signal as flat). The hypothesis is that bullish SuperTrend flips that co-occur with above-average ADX and volume, occurring above the BMSB (Bull Market Support Band), carry a positive expected return over the subsequent 24-72 hours in spot BTC/ETH/altcoin markets because they capture breakouts out of ATR-anchored consolidations. However, given the 0.3% positive-fitness rate in prior evolution, this hypothesis is weak and requires strong additional confirmation filters (RSI, BMSB, bb_bw_percentile expansion) to be viable. Classification as doubtful reflects this uncertainty.
market_techniques: ATR-trailing SuperTrend flip as trend-resumption signal (long-only bullish flips only)
ADX trend-strength gate to suppress whipsaws in ranging markets
Volatility-percentile regime filter (bb_bw_percentile) to require bandwidth expansion at entry — avoids entering during tight squeezes where flips are noise
BMSB macro filter: only enter longs when price is above both BMSB SMA and EMA — structurally bullish market regime
Volume confirmation: volume_ratio > threshold at flip bar to require institutional participation
ATR-scaled stop-loss and take-profit to make risk levels adaptive to realized volatility
Time stop to cap exposure when the trend fails to develop within 48 hours
RSI momentum confirmation: avoid entries when rsi_14 is already overbought (>70) at flip — prevents buying exhaustion tops
Cooldown after stop-loss hit to prevent re-entry into whipsawing market
Walk-forward expanding-window optimization on pre-lockbox data only
useful_features: trend: adx_14 — primary trend-strength filter, keep ADX floor in sweep
trend: bmsb_bullish (bool) — structural macro bull filter; long only when price is above both 20-SMA and 21-EMA bands
volatility: atr_14 — used internally for SuperTrend bands and for SL/TP sizing
volatility: bb_bw_percentile — bandwidth percentile rank; require >40 to confirm volatility expansion at flip, reducing noise-flip entries
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass vol as alternative volatility regime filter
volume: volume_ratio — volume vs 20-bar SMA, existing filter; consider raising threshold
volume: volume_zscore — z-score vs 50-bar rolling; spike confirmation
volume: obv_divergence — OBV divergence from its SMA as secondary volume trend filter
momentum: rsi_14 — overbought guard: skip longs if rsi_14 > 70 at entry to avoid buying tops
momentum: roc_12 — rate of change over 12 bars; only enter if roc_12 > 0 (price accelerating upward)
momentum: price_acceleration — second derivative of price; positive value at flip confirms impulse
mean-reversion: dist_from_high_20 — avoid entries when price is already stretched near 20-bar high (>-2%)
market regime: _regime column (trend/breakout/range/bear) — existing filter; tighten to trend-only or add breakout only when bb_bw_percentile > 50
microstructure: pressure_imbalance — buying minus selling pressure proxy; require positive imbalance at flip bar
microstructure: close_location — bar close in upper half of range at flip bar confirms bullish close

genome_params.name: multiplier
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for SuperTrend bands. Wider = fewer flips. Current evolved value 3.9 is near the high end; allow GA to explore 2.0-5.0.

genome_params.name: atr_period
genome_params.note: Period for ATR calculation inside SuperTrend. Shorter = more sensitive. Fixed at 14 in current code; open to sweep.

genome_params.name: adx_min
genome_params.note: ADX floor for trend strength filter. Current evolved 17.2; allow broader sweep to find optimal gate.

genome_params.name: volume_threshold
genome_params.note: volume_ratio threshold. Current 1.06 (near-average); GA should explore stricter confirmation up to 2.0x.

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for initial stop-loss below entry. Current 3.0; tighten floor to 1.5 for better R:R.

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for take-profit target. Must remain > stop_loss_atr_mult. Current 5.8; wide range is acceptable for trend trades.

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Maximum hours in position before forced exit. Current 48h; allow sweep up to 120h (5 days) for longer trend trades.

genome_params.name: rsi_overbought_cap
genome_params.note: New param: skip long entries when rsi_14 exceeds this threshold at flip bar. Prevents buying exhaustion.

genome_params.name: bb_bw_pct_min
genome_params.note: New param: minimum bb_bw_percentile at entry bar. Requires volatility expansion at flip, reducing noise entries.

genome_params.name: require_bmsb
genome_params.note: New param: if True, only enter longs when bmsb_bullish is True (price above both BMSB bands). Structural bull filter.

do_not_optimize: allowed_regimes — fixing to [trend, breakout] is conceptually correct; letting the GA choose regime sets creates enormous regime-label leakage and overfitting to historical regime labels
require_trend_regime — always True; removing the regime gate turns the strategy into an always-on flipper which will be overfit to trend periods in IS data
require_volume — always True in long-only spot; volume confirmation is a fundamental quality filter and should not be an evolvable boolean

atr_period when jointly sweeping multiplier — the two parameters are strongly collinear (wider ATR period smooths the band equivalent to a larger multiplier); sweeping both simultaneously invites a degenerate solution space with many equivalent configurations

risk_management.stop: ATR-scaled initial hard stop: entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14, where stop_loss_atr_mult in [1.5, 4.0]. Checked against bar low each bar (current backtester implementation). For spot: stop triggers a full exit to cash, no re-entry for cooldown_hours.
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-scaled fixed take-profit: entry_price + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14, where take_profit_atr_mult > stop_loss_atr_mult. Checked against bar high. Wide TPs (5-8x ATR) preferred for trend trades to allow the trend to run.
risk_management.trailing: Optional trailing stop: after price moves 1.5x ATR in favor, trail the stop at 1.0x ATR below the rolling high. Implemented externally; the current backtester does not support trailing natively — add as a post-entry adjustment each bar when unrealized PnL > 1.5 ATR.
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-scaled sizing using the backtester's volatility_scaled sizing_method: target 15-20% annualized portfolio volatility. Position size = equity * (vol_target / realized_vol). Cap at max_position_pct. This prevents oversizing during low-volatility regimes where ATR is compressed.
risk_management.max_size: Maximum 25% of equity per position (max_position_pct=0.25). Spot-only, no leverage. Circuit breaker halts new entries if drawdown exceeds 15% from peak.
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 6-hour cooldown after any stop-loss hit before re-entry is permitted (min_time_between_trades in RiskConfig). Prevents whipsaw sequences. Extend to 24 hours after two consecutive stop-outs.
risk_management.risk_off: Regime gate: require _regime in [trend, breakout]. Additionally gate on bmsb_bullish=True as macro filter. If adx_14 < 15 for the last 5 bars (range regime confirmed), suppress all new entries regardless of SuperTrend flip. Do not enter during bear regimes.

metrics: CAGR on OOS lockbox (2024-06-07 to 2026-06-07) — primary performance metric
Sharpe ratio (annualized, risk-free=0) on OOS equity curve — target > 1.0
Sortino ratio (downside deviation only) — target > 1.3, more appropriate for one-sided spot exposure
Maximum drawdown % (peak-to-trough) on full OOS period — target < 20%
Hit rate (% winning trades) — expect 35-50% for trend-following with wide TP; low hit rate with high avg win is acceptable
Turnover (trades per month) — expect 3-8 trades/month given wide multiplier; too-low turnover reduces statistical power
Average trade duration (hours) — validate against time_stop_hours; should see bimodal distribution (quick stops vs TP hits)
Exposure % (fraction of time in position) — expect 20-40% for long-only trend strategy with strong filters
Average winning trade / average losing trade ratio — target > 1.8 to compensate for sub-50% hit rate
Cost sensitivity: rerun OOS with 2x commission and 2x slippage to verify edge survives realistic friction (69 trades * 2 legs * ~0.1% = ~14% gross PnL consumed by costs at baseline)
Walk-forward Sharpe stability (per-fold coefficient of variation < 0.5) — checks that performance does not come from one lucky fold
Bootstrap/Monte Carlo: 1000 resamplings of trade sequence to get 95% CI on Sharpe and maxDD (already in auto_evolve for top genomes)
Per-year CAGR breakdown (2024, 2025, first half 2026) — must be positive in at least 2 of 3 years to avoid single-year fluke
OOS degradation % vs IS walk-forward Sharpe — flag if degradation > 50% (strategy is overfit)
sweep_changes: Convert short branch to flat: in supertrend_strategy, change line 132 from `side = 'long' if dir_now == 1 else 'short'` to `if dir_now != 1: return None` and always set side='long'. Remove the short-side stop/TP block (lines 144-146).
Add rsi_overbought_cap filter: after the existing ADX and volume checks, add `if current.get('rsi_14', 50) > p['rsi_overbought_cap']: return None` to prevent buying into exhaustion.
Add bb_bw_percentile expansion filter: `if current.get('bb_bw_percentile', 50) < p['bb_bw_pct_min']: return None` to require volatility expansion at the flip bar.
Add BMSB macro filter (optional param): `if p['require_bmsb'] and not current.get('bmsb_bullish', True): return None` to restrict longs to structurally bullish market phases.
Add rsi_overbought_cap to PARAMS default (75.0) and to STRATEGY_REGISTRY param_space: ('float', 65.0, 80.0).
Add bb_bw_pct_min to PARAMS default (35.0) and to STRATEGY_REGISTRY param_space: ('float', 30.0, 60.0).
Add require_bmsb to PARAMS default (True) and to STRATEGY_REGISTRY param_space: ('bool',).

Re-add supertrend to STRATEGY_REGISTRY with the updated long-only param_space covering: multiplier [2.0, 5.0], atr_period [7, 21], adx_min [15.0, 35.0], volume_threshold [0.8, 2.0], stop_loss_atr_mult [1.5, 4.0], take_profit_atr_mult [3.0, 8.0], time_stop_hours [24, 120], rsi_overbought_cap [65.0, 80.0], bb_bw_pct_min [30.0, 60.0], require_bmsb (bool).

Set minimum trade count threshold to 40 for this strategy in fitness evaluation (currently 30 global) — given the 69-trade IS result with a wide multiplier, the genome needs enough flips to be statistically meaningful.

Add cooldown enforcement: track last_exit_reason in metadata; if stop_loss, suppress new signals for at least 6 hours (via time_between_trades in RiskConfig or a bar counter in the strategy function itself).

Do NOT add supertrend back to the active registry for standard evolution until the long-only conversion is verified against a 6-month pre-lockbox validation window — the 0.3% positive-fitness rate from 36K evals is a strong prior that should not be ignored without new evidence.
methodological_risks: Survivorship bias in the evolved PARAMS: the single surviving genome from 36K evaluations (Sharpe=1.20, 69 trades) may represent an extreme overfit to a specific trend period in IS data. The small trade count means that a handful of high-PnL trades drive the entire backlog.

Low positive-fitness rate (0.3% from 36K evaluations) constitutes strong empirical evidence that the signal lacks a consistent edge in this market. Converting to long-only does not fix the fundamental signal quality — it only removes one broken branch. The prior probability that long-only variant is viable is very low.

SuperTrend's bar-by-bar recompute with a 200-bar lookback cap causes the computed band and direction to drift vs a full-history computation, especially early in the lookback window. This creates inconsistent signal behavior that makes IS/OOS comparison unreliable — the IS Sharpe may reflect a slightly different indicator than the OOS one.

ADX filter at 17.2 is so loose that it barely filters anything. If the GA finds 17.2 as the 'optimal' floor, it means the ADX filter has zero discrimination power at that level. A loose threshold fitted to noise is worse than no threshold.

The volume_threshold of 1.06 (slightly above average) means the volume filter is nearly always satisfied. Both ADX and volume filters evolved to near-trivial thresholds, suggesting the GA was not actually selecting for quality entries but rather maximizing the number of trades.

Regime label leakage: the _regime column is itself derived from historical feature windows that overlap training data. If regime labels are computed on the full dataset before the walk-forward split (which is common), they constitute a form of lookahead across fold boundaries.

The 48-hour time stop combined with the entry lag of 1 bar means a flip on bar N is entered at bar N+1's open and exits at most 48 hours later. In hourly data this is 48 bars. Given SuperTrend's lagging nature, the impulsive move that caused the flip has typically already occurred within the first few bars — the time stop may be capturing mean-reversion, not trend continuation.
Monte Carlo ruin probability not reported for the existing genome — with only 69 trades, bootstrap CIs on Sharpe will be very wide, meaning the 1.20 IS Sharpe could plausibly be noise.

Cost sensitivity is a significant concern: 69 trades * 2 legs * ~10bps commission + slippage = roughly 10-15% of gross PnL consumed by costs. Any reduction in edge from the long-only conversion (losing the short-side PnL, which was ~50% of trades) must be compensated by higher per-trade quality.

The per-bar SuperTrend recomputation is O(n) inside the bar loop, making the strategy O(n^2) total. At 200-bar lookback and hourly data over 5 years, this is manageable, but in the GA with 36K evaluations it represents significant wasted compute for a signal that ultimately fails.
